question: How do I move my online store from Shopify over to Wix? I have about 200 products with images and descriptions and I really don't want to re-enter all of that by hand.
answer:
You can migrate your Shopify store to Wix Stores using the Cart2Cart app, which transfers products, orders, and coupons — no manual re-entry. The app is free to add, but Cart2Cart charges a fee for the migration; you see an exact price breakdown in a preview before you commit. As a free DIY alternative, you can import your products directly through a CSV file.

Before you begin: add Wix Stores to your site, and to migrate store orders, upgrade to a Premium or Studio plan that allows you to accept payments.

1. Go to the Wix App Market and add the **Cart2Cart** app, sign in or create a Cart2Cart account, and make sure the email associated with the relevant Wix account is selected.
2. Click **Source and Target Cart Setup**: select **Shopify** from the Source Cart drop-down, enter your store's URL, and paste your Shopify API password (create it in Shopify under Apps > Manage private apps with Read Access permissions).
3. Select **Wix** from the Target Cart drop-down and click **Choose Entities** to pick what to migrate, then run the demo migration to preview the result and the exact cost before performing the full migration.

question: i wanna transfer my domain away from wix to namecheap how do i get the auth code
answer:
You get the transfer authorization (EPP) code from your Wix account:

1. Go to **Domains** in your Wix account.
2. Click the **Domain Actions** icon next to the relevant domain and select **Transfer away from Wix**.
3. After reviewing the info, click **Transfer Domain**, then **I Still Want to Transfer**.

The EPP code is sent to your domain's registrant contact email address. Once you have it, follow Namecheap's instructions to initiate the transfer on their end; you can confirm initiation on whois.com, where the domain's Status field shows "pending transfer".

Notes: ICANN rules block transfers within 60 days of purchase (and changing registrant contact info locks the domain for 60 days); the transfer can take up to 7 days; after the transfer your Wix site is disconnected from the domain and renewals are billed by your new host.

question: We're migrating our company website from WordPress to Wix. Does Wix have a tool that pulls in our existing pages and media library, or is it a manual copy-paste job?
answer:
There is no tool that imports a full site created outside of Wix — that is currently not supported, so your existing pages need to be rebuilt in the Wix editor. What you can import automatically is your blog: Wix Blog imports published posts from WordPress (WordPress.com or WordPress.org), including post text, images, videos, original post dates and times, alt text, and categories, up to 4,000 posts per site. Add Wix Blog to your site first, then run the import.

Not imported: drafts and scheduled posts, contact forms, author names, tags, custom plugins, manually inserted code, PDF files and other attachments, and comments. After importing, set up 301 redirects from the old post URLs to the new ones to keep your SEO ranking.

question: My domain is at GoDaddy and I keep getting errors when I try to connect it to Wix. Do I need to change the nameservers or just add records, and how long does it take to work?
answer:
Both methods work — pick one and set it up fully (errors usually come from mixing the two):

**Name servers** (Wix manages your DNS): In Wix, go to **Domains** > **Add an Existing Domain** > **Connect a domain you already own**, select the site, enter your domain, click **Yes, I own this domain**, then **Start**. Then in GoDaddy: click your profile > **My Products** > **DNS** next to your domain > **Change** in the name servers section > **Enter my own name servers (advanced)**, and replace them with the exact NS records shown on your Wix domains page.

**Pointing** (DNS stays at GoDaddy): In GoDaddy's DNS settings, edit (or add) the A record with **@** in the Name column to point to **185.230.63.107**, and edit (or add) the CNAME record with **www** in the Name column to point to **pointing.wixdns.net**.

In both cases your Wix site must be upgraded to a plan first, and changes can take up to 48 hours to propagate. If you have Office 365 by GoDaddy email or DNS records you don't want to change, use pointing.

question: your f***ing form builder is garbage. filled it out to test and no email came to my inbox. i need the notifications to actually send when someone submits how do i fix that
answer:
To get form submission notifications arriving in your inbox:

1. Whitelist the following in your email client: the domains **@automations.ascendbywix.com**, ***@crm.wix.com** and ***@wixforms.com** (include the asterisk so all variants are whitelisted), and the email address **no-reply@test.wixshoutout.com**.
2. Republish your site after whitelisting.

By default, when someone submits a form an email is automatically sent to the site owner via a Wix Automation. You can edit that automation to choose who gets notified — the site owner or other email addresses, including external ones — and you can add a **Show in Inbox** action to also receive the submission in your Wix Inbox.

question: why the h*ll did my About page just vanish from the menu?? it was there yesterday. i didnt touch a d*mn thing. get it back
answer:
The page was most likely hidden from the menu — hidden pages still exist and can be restored in a few clicks:

1. Go to your editor.
2. Click **Pages & Menu** on the left side of the editor.
3. Click the **More Actions** icon next to the About page — a **Hidden** icon indicates hidden pages in this panel.
4. Select **Show in menu**.

The page and its content are untouched; hiding only removes it from the site menu. Note that hiding a page on desktop does not hide it on the mobile version, and hidden pages still appear in search engines.
